headerDisplay: On/off (privacy) control for TP-Link Kasa cameras. Enter your TP-Link account email + password and list each camera's local IP — the plugin auto-detects the rest. Control is routed through the TP-Link cloud (Kasa camera firmware has no local control path); on/off **state** is read locally over UDP. No app capture or rooted device needed.

ffmpegPath.title: ffmpeg path
ffmpegPath.description: Path to ffmpeg (required only for camera video). Many Homebridge setups already bundle one.

copyVideo.title: Copy video (no transcode)
copyVideo.description: Pass the source H.264 through without re-encoding (much lower CPU). Turn off if video is glitchy in HomeKit.

privacyAsSwitch.title: Privacy as a separate switch
privacyAsSwitch.description: Disable HomeKit Secure Video and expose on/off as a standalone switch instead of the camera tile's native Camera Off control. Enable if HKSV/native controls cause issues.

exposeLed.title: Expose status-LED switch
exposeLed.description: Adds a switch to toggle each camera's status LED. The camera on/off (privacy) switch is always exposed.

exposeMotionDetection.title: Expose motion-detection switch
exposeMotionDetection.description: Adds a switch to enable/disable each camera's motion detection.

ip.title: Local IP
ip.description: Camera's LAN IP. deviceId, model and name are auto-detected from it.

source.title: ffmpeg source args (enables video)
source.description: ffmpeg input args for the live feed, e.g. '-rtsp_transport tcp -i rtsp://<go2rtc-host>:8554/living_room'. When set, a HomeKit camera tile is added. Requires ffmpeg.

stillImageSource.title: ffmpeg still-image args (optional)
stillImageSource.description: ffmpeg input args for snapshots, e.g. '-i http://<go2rtc-host>:1984/api/frame.jpeg?src=living_room'. Defaults to the source.

deviceId.title: Device ID (optional)
deviceId.description: Only needed if the camera isn't reachable on the local network.
